Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 839,889.57 moved across the accounts in this case.
  • The largest source of funds was DEEPAK SINGH, which sent out Rs 273,350.80.
  • Money was routed through 1 intermediary account.
  • ASHOK ASHOK PANDEY spread money to 113 different accounts.
  • ASHOK ASHOK PANDEY collected money from 46 different accounts.
  • Rs 3,649,584.18 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals.
  • The account to investigate first is YASH DUBEY.

YASH DUBEY strong
acct_001 · DCB Bank · Account Number 25078124219247 · Score 2,562
Rank #1

The account of YASH DUBEY (25078124219247) exhibits suspicious money behavior, characterized by a large credit of 400000.00 and subsequent debits, as well as large transfers to unrelated parties. This pattern suggests potential money laundering or other illicit activities. The account's transactions warrant further investigation due to the presence of multiple strong patterns and distinct patterns.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 400000.00 on 2025-02-01 was traced into 17 subsequent debits, with the entire amount allocated and no remaining balance, indicating a potentially suspicious money trail. (+23 more of this type)
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of 50001.00 occurred on 2025-04-10 from YASH DUBEY (25078124219247) to MR. VIKRAM NIKHIL MALHOTRA (700870937566), with no prior observed relationship between the parties, raising suspicions of unusual or illicit activity. (+1 more of this type)
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Transactions acct_001_004883 and acct_001_004885 share similar characteristics, including account, direction, amount, date, and narration, but were not flagged as duplicates, potentially indicating an oversight or manipulation in the transaction recording process. (+2 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 221 nodes and 415 directed transaction edges was constructed, indicating a complex network of transactions that may be worthy of further examination for potential money laundering or other financial crimes.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 7 suspicious round-value debits totaling 267000.00, which may suggest a pattern of structured transactions designed to avoid detection or reporting thresholds.

The graph shows a timeline of suspicious activity from 2023-11-20 to 2025-09-29, covering a total of 11869 transactions. An investigator should take note of the time span, approximately 22 months, and the fact that no transactions were flagged or excluded, indicating that all 11869 transactions are under scrutiny.

Fraud-Pattern Summary

The graph shows various fraud patterns, with the most notable being Duplicate Verification at 273 occurrences. The busiest pattern is Duplicate Verification, which significantly outnumbers other patterns like Failed Reversed Transaction Detection at 4 and Money Trail Tracing at 40. An investigator should take note of these figures to identify potential areas of fraud, particularly the high frequency of Duplicate Verification. The time span is not specified, but the data provides insight into the relative frequencies of different patterns.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
